text.title: Review Scenarios with Suggested Patches
text.summary: Concrete examples of bug, design, security, and performance review comments with actionable patches.

example.note: discount_percent is treated as a multiplier (20x) instead of a percentage (20%).

example.code: def calculate_discount(price: float, discount_percent: float) -> float: """ Args: discount_percent: percentage as whole number (e.g., 20 for 20%) """ return price - (price * discount_percent / 100) # calculate_discount(100, 20) returns 80

example.note: No authentication, no authorization. Sequential user IDs are trivially enumerable.

example.code: def get_user_orders(user_id: int) -> list[dict]: user = ( db.query(User) .options(selectinload(User.orders).selectinload(Order.items)) .filter(User.id == user_id) .first() ) return [{"id": o.id, "items": [i.name for i in o.items]} for o in user.orders] # Now: 1 user query + 1 orders query + 1 items query = 3 total
